title: Sr Software Engineer
description:
The Supervisor Tech space is a combination of teams and technologies that build and operate tech to support managers, team leads in Uber's COE and BPOs. The supporting tech is mission critical in ensuring we are assigning and completing the work to the right staff and ensure the measurements are tracked with realtime Insights. The engineer on this team will work with other engineers in the team to build out scalable solutions to enable more features to the above mentioned persona.
   

  

 We are looking for someone who has a passion for designing and driving projects from inception to production. The person will work with stakeholders, product managers, data scientists, and partner engineering teams to gather requirements and translate them into a platform feature. Also join on call rotation, driving continuous improvements on system availability, scalability, performance and efficiency.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* 5\\+ years of experience in software development.
* Scalable system architecture design
* Production\\-quality services, deployment, monitoring, and reliability.
* Microservice designs, gRPC or REST API development
* Ability to decompose complex business problems into clean, extensible, performant services and systems
* Logging, monitoring, performance troubleshooting and tuning experience
* Excellent problem\\-solving skills and the ability to work in a collaborative team environment
* Comfortable in navigating multi\\-disciplinary teams with respect
* Focus on code quality in terms of coverage, code reviews
* Excellent technical writing skills to communicate design, requirements, flows, and architecture
* Fluent in one of these programming languages: Golang, Java,
* Solid understanding of algorithms, data structures, and software design principles.
* Experience with version control systems such as Git.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Exposure to machine learning, GenAI, or LLM technologies
* Proven experience in designing and developing scalable microservices aligned with modern architectural principles and best practices.

title: ETL & Python Testing

**What You'll Love About Working With Us**

* Opportunities for career development, upskilling, and certifications across emerging technologies

 Capgemini in India comprises nearly 235,000 team members working across 13 locations: Bangalore, Bhubaneswar, Chennai, Coimbatore, Gandhinagar, Gurugram, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Mumbai, Noida, Pune, Salem, and Tiruchirappalli.

title: Engineer, Associate

title: Group Digital Development Tools Engineer - Associate Consultant - MFT - KGS CH
description:
**Job Description**
**Job Title**
 Development Tools Engineer – Grade E
   

  

**Country**
 Offshore (aligned to UK service ownership and operating hours)
   

  

**Service Line Group**
 Group Digital
   

  

**KPMG Overview**
 Cloud Engineering within Group Digital provides build and run capabilities for enterprise cloud platforms. This includes platform automation, DevOps tooling, CI/CD enablement, security baseline implementation, and operational support across Azure, AWS, and Google Cloud.
   

  

 The function also provides shared development tooling capabilities that support engineering teams across the Firm. These tools enable secure software development, automated deployment, and operational consistency across multiple technology platforms.
   

  

 The services are already in active use across internal delivery teams. The focus is on stability, standardisation, and controlled improvement of the development platform ecosystem.
   

  

**Service Line Information**
 Cloud Engineering services the broader Firm through delivery of core technology and managed services capabilities, collaboration and innovation development services, and development of strategic technology partnerships.
   

  

 The Development Tools team supports the engineering community by providing and operating enterprise development platforms, CI/CD tooling, and supporting automation services that underpin software delivery across the Firm.
   

  

**Job Description**
**The Team**
 The role sits within the Cloud Engineering / Development Tools team in Group Digital. The team is responsible for engineering, maintaining, and operating the development tooling ecosystem used by multiple engineering teams.
   

  

 The team supports enterprise tooling platforms including Azure DevOps, GitHub, Docker registries, Atlassian tooling, mobile developer consoles, and infrastructure automation tooling.
   

  

 These services underpin large numbers of active engineering teams and production workloads. The focus of the team is on operational stability, platform standardisation, and continuous improvement of the developer experience through automation and reliable tooling.
   

  

 The team operates within a multi\\-supplier delivery model and works closely with cloud platform teams, security teams, and application delivery teams.
   

  

**The Role**
 The Development Tools Engineer supports the operation and maintenance of enterprise development tooling platforms.
   

  

 The role focuses on maintaining development platform configurations, supporting CI/CD pipelines, and assisting engineering teams using the development tools ecosystem.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
**Key Responsibilities**
* Support operation of development platforms including Azure DevOps and GitHub
* Maintain CI/CD pipelines and development tooling configurations
* Manage repository structures, permissions, and development workflows
* Support container tooling including Docker
* Assist with troubleshooting issues affecting development platforms
* Maintain development tooling documentation and support materials
* Support secure configuration of development environments


**Qualifications**
**The Person**
 To be effective in this role, the individual must have:
   

  

* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or a related technical discipline
* 2–4 years of professional experience in DevOps engineering, development tooling support, or platform engineering roles
* Experience working with enterprise development platforms or source control systems supporting engineering teams
* Experience supporting CI/CD pipelines and build automation workflows within development environments
* Experience operating within enterprise engineering environments with structured configuration management and controlled platform updates
* Experience assisting development teams with tooling configuration, troubleshooting, and developer workflow support
* Experience working with development platforms such as GitHub or Azure DevOps
* Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices
* Familiarity with container tooling such as Docker
* Experience supporting developer platforms or tooling environments
* Strong troubleshooting and communication skills


**Preferred Certifications**
* Microsoft Certified: Azure DevOps Engineer Expert
* GitHub Foundations Certification
* HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate


 This role directly supports the engineering productivity and operational reliability of the Firm’s development platform ecosystem.
   

  

 Effective execution of this role improves developer productivity, reduces friction in the software delivery lifecycle, enforces platform security standards, and ensures that development teams can build and deploy software within a controlled and supportable enterprise environment.

title: Principal Engineer
description:
**Job Description**
****Principal Software Engineer (P6\\)****
**Join us at Zendesk, where we're on a mission to power exceptional service for every person on the planet. We're accelerating that ambition by building products rooted in AI, automation, and intelligent customer experiences, because behind every interaction lies an opportunity to make a human connection.**
**We’re establishing a world\\-class Research \\& Development centre in Pune and are hiring a Principal Software Engineer to lead technical direction for high\\-impact platform and product initiatives. In this role you’ll design and deliver large\\-scale systems, influence cross\\-team architecture, and mentor senior engineers so Zendesk can scale reliably while embedding AI\\-enabled innovation into our customer experience.**
**Summary**
**As a Principal Software Engineer you will own large, cross\\-cutting technical areas and drive architectural decisions that affect multiple teams and products. You’ll partner closely with Product, Design, SRE, and Engineering leaders to define roadmaps, reduce delivery risk, and deliver scalable, secure, and highly available systems. You will also mentor senior engineers and help grow technical leadership across the organization.**
****What you’ll be doing****
* Own and drive architecture for complex systems or platform capabilities that serve multiple product teams; define APIs, interfaces, and abstractions that enable scale and reuse.
* Lead end\\-to\\-end delivery of large features and platform initiatives: design, implementation, testing, rollout, and operational readiness.
* Set technical direction and standards: guide design reviews, influence roadmap trade\\-offs, and promote engineering excellence across teams.
* Champion an “AI\\-First” mindset: guide teams to thoughtfully integrate AI into product features and internal workflows to deliver purposeful, high\\-value automation and innovation.
* Improve reliability and resilience: define SLOs/SLIs, lead incident retrospectives, reduce operational toil, and advance disaster recovery readiness.
* Mentor senior engineers, staff\\-level contributors, and architects; develop technical talents and raise overall engineering craft.
* Collaborate with globally distributed cross\\-functional partners (Product, Design, Security, SRE, Program Management) to balance business value, risk mitigation, and technical debt.
* Advocate for and implement scalable observability, testing, CI/CD, and secure engineering practices.


****What you bring to the role****
* Technical authority and depth: you have a proven history of designing and delivering large\\-scale, distributed systems with measurable impact.
* AI\\-first perspective: you can identify high\\-leverage opportunities for responsible AI adoption, guiding teams to integrate ML/AI where it delivers clear product or operational value.
* Systems and architectural thinking: strong skills in system design, data consistency models, performance and capacity planning, and trade\\-off analysis.
* Cross\\-team influence: experience aligning multiple teams and stakeholders on technical strategy, standards, and roadmaps without direct reporting authority.
* Coaching and mentorship orientation: you actively develop other senior engineers and technical leaders through feedback, code/design reviews, and role modeling.
* Product empathy and pragmatism: you balance speed, maintainability, and user outcomes, making pragmatic decisions that serve customers and the business.
* Operational excellence mindset: you prioritize observability, reliability, incident readiness, and measurable SLAs/SLOs.
* Clear communicator and collaborator: you present technical trade\\-offs to technical and non\\-technical audiences, and you build trust across Product, Security, and Business teams.


**Required Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.
* 10\\+ years of professional software engineering experience, with demonstrated ownership of large production systems.
* 4\\+ years in senior technical leadership roles (senior engineer, staff, principal) with cross\\-team impact.
* Strong software design and system architecture skills: distributed systems, data consistency, performance and capacity planning.
* Proven proficiency in one or more production languages (examples: Go, Java, Python, Ruby, TypeScript) and experience with cloud platforms (GCP/AWS/Azure).
